questions.question_text: Find the H.C.F. of $2^3 \times 3^2 \times 5 \times 7^4$, $2^2 \times 3^5 \times 5^2 \times 7^3$, $2^3 \times 5^3 \times 7^2$.
questions.solution:
The prime numbers common to given numbers are 2, 5 and 7.
∴ H.C.F. = $2^2 \times 5 \times 7^2 = 980$.

questions.question_text:
Find the H.C.F. of:
(a) 42, 63 and 140
(b) 108, 288 and 360
Find the H.C.F. of 513, 1134 and 1215.
Reduce $\frac{391}{667}$ to lowest terms.
questions.solution:
H.C.F. of 391 and 667 is 23.
On dividing the numerator and denominator by 23, we get:
$\frac{391 \div 23}{667 \div 23} = \frac{17}{29}$

questions.question_text: Find the L.C.M. of $2^2 \times 3^3 \times 5 \times 7^2$, $2^3 \times 3^2 \times 5^2 \times 7^4$, $2 \times 3 \times 5^3 \times 7 \times 11$.
questions.solution: L.C.M. = Product of highest powers of 2, 3, 5, 7 and 11 = $2^3 \times 3^3 \times 5^3 \times 7^4 \times 11$.

questions.question_text:
Find the L.C.M. of
(a) 87 and 145
(b) 72, 108 and 2100
Find the L.C.M. of 16, 24, 36 and 54.
Find the H.C.F. and L.C.M. of $\frac{2}{3}, \frac{8}{9}, \frac{16}{81}$ and $\frac{10}{27}$.
questions.solution:
H.C.F. of given fractions = $\frac{\text{H.C.F. of 2, 8, 16, 10}}{\text{L.C.M. of 3, 9, 81, 27}} = \frac{2}{81}$.
L.C.M. of given fractions = $\frac{\text{L.C.M. of 2, 8, 16, 10}}{\text{H.C.F. of 3, 9, 81, 27}} = \frac{80}{3}$.

questions.question_text: Find the H.C.F. and L.C.M. of 0.63, 1.05 and 2.1.
questions.solution:
Making the same number of decimal places, the given numbers are 0.63, 1.05 and 2.10.
Without decimal places, these numbers are 63, 105 and 210.
Now, H.C.F. of 63, 105 and 210 is 21.
∴ H.C.F. of 0.63, 1.05 and 2.1 is 0.21.
L.C.M. of 63, 105 and 210 is 630.
∴ L.C.M. of 0.63, 1.05 and 2.1 is 6.30.

questions.question_text: Two numbers are in the ratio of 15: 11. If their H.C.F. is 13, find the numbers.
questions.solution:
Let the required numbers be 15x and 11x.
Then, their H.C.F. is x. So, x = 13.
∴ The numbers are ($15 \times 13$ and $11 \times 13$), i.e., 195 and 143.

questions.question_text: Two numbers are in the ratio of 3: 4. Their L.C.M. is 84. Find the numbers.
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be 3x and 4x. Then, their L.C.M. = 12x.
So, 12x = 84 or x = 7.
∴ The numbers are 21 and 28.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of two numbers is 11 and their L.C.M. is 693. If one of the numbers is 77, find the other.
questions.solution: Other number = $\left(\frac{11 \times 693}{77}\right) = 99$.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 462 and their highest common factor is 22. What is the minimum number of pairs that satisfy these conditions?
questions.solution:
Let the required numbers be 22a and 22b.
Then, $22a + 22b = 462 \Rightarrow a + b = 21$.
Now, co-primes with sum 21 are (1, 20), (2, 19), (4, 17), (5, 16), (8, 13) and (10, 11).
∴ Required numbers are ($22 \times 1, 22 \times 20$), ($22 \times 2, 22 \times 19$),
($22 \times 4, 22 \times 17$), ($22 \times 5, 22 \times 16$), ($22 \times 8, 22 \times 13$) and ($22 \times 10, 22 \times 11$).
Clearly, the number of such pairs is 6.

questions.question_text: The sum and difference of the L.C.M and H.C.F. of two numbers are 592 and 518 respectively. If the sum of the numbers be 296, find the numbers.
questions.solution:
Let L and H denote the L.C.M and H.C.F of the two numbers.
Then, $L + H = 592$ ...(i) And, $L - H = 518$ ....(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get: $2L = 1110$ or $L = 555$.
∴ $H = 592 - 555 = 37$.
So, H.C.F. = 37 and L.C.M. = 555.
Let the numbers be x and $(296 - x)$.
Then, $x(296 - x) = 555 \times 37 \Rightarrow x^2 - 296x + 20535 = 0$
$\Rightarrow x^2 - 185x - 111x + 20535 = 0 \Rightarrow x(x - 185) - 111(x - 185) = 0$
$\Rightarrow (x - 185)(x - 111) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 185$ or $x = 111$.
Hence the numbers are 111 and 185.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest possible length which can be used to measure exactly the lengths 4 m 95 cm, 9 m and 16 m 65 cm.
questions.solution:
Required length = H.C.F. of 495 cm, 900 cm and 1665 cm.
$495 = 3^2 \times 5 \times 11$, $900 = 2^2 \times 3^2 \times 5^2$, $1665 = 3^2 \times 5 \times 37$.
∴ H.C.F. = $3^2 \times 5 = 45$.
Hence, required length = 45 cm.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest number which on dividing 1657 and 2037 leaves remainders 6 and 5 respectively.
Find the largest number which divides 62, 132 and 237 to leave the same remainder in each case.
The H.C.F. of two numbers, each having three digits is 17 and their L.C.M. is 714. Find the sum of the numbers.
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be 17a and 17b.
Then, $17a \times 17b = 17 \times 714 \Rightarrow ab = 42$.
Now, co-primes with product 42 are (1, 42), (2, 21), (3, 14) and (6, 7).
So, the numbers are ($17 \times 1, 17 \times 42$), ($17 \times 2, 17 \times 21$), ($17 \times 3, 17 \times 14$) and ($17 \times 6, 17 \times 7$).
Since both the numbers are 3-digit numbers, the suitable pair is ($17 \times 6, 17 \times 7$), i.e., (102, 119).
∴ Required sum = $102 + 119 = 221$.

questions.question_text: Find the least number which is exactly divisible by 32, 36, 45, 60 and 80.
Find the least number which when divided by 6, 7, 8, 9 and 12 leaves the same remainder 1 in each case.
Find the smallest number which when increased by 10 is completely divisible by 12, 15, 18, 20 and 24.
questions.solution:
Required number
= (L.C.M. of 12, 15, 18, 20, 24) - 10
= ($2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 5 \times 3 \times 2$) - 10
= $360 - 10 = 350$.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest number of five digits which is divisible by 15, 21 and 36.
questions.solution:
Greatest number of five digits = 99999.
Required number must be divisible by L.C.M. of 15, 21, 36, i.e., 1260.
On dividing 99999 by 1260, we get 459 as remainder.
∴ Required number = $(99999 - 459) = 99540$.

questions.question_text: Find the smallest number of five digits exactly divisible by 16, 24, 36 and 54.
questions.solution:
Smallest number of five digits is 10000.
Required number must be divisible by L.C.M. of 16, 24, 36, 54, i.e., 432.
On dividing 10000 by 432, we get 64 as remainder.
∴ Required number = $10000 + (432 - 64) = 10368$.

questions.question_text: Find the largest number which when subtracted from 10000, the remainder is divisible by 32, 36, 48 and 50.
questions.solution:
Required number
= 10000 - (L.C.M. of 32, 36, 48, 50)
= 10000 - ($2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 3$)
= $10000 - 864 = 9136$.

questions.question_text: Find the largest number of five digits which, when divided by 16, 24, 30 or 36, leaves the same remainder 10 in each case.
questions.solution:
Largest number of 5 digits = 99999. L.C.M. of 16, 24, 30 and 36 = 720.
On dividing 99999 by 720, remainder obtained is 639.
\ Largest number of 5 digits divisible by 16, 24, 30 and 36 = (99999 - 639) = 99360.
Hence, required number = (99360 + 10) = 99370.

questions.question_text: Find the least number which when divided by 20, 25, 35 and 40 leaves remainders 14, 19, 29 and 34 respectively.
What is the least number which when divided by the numbers 3, 5, 6, 8, 10 and 12 leaves in each case a remainder 2 but when divided by 13 leaves no remainder?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 3, 5, 6, 8, 10 and 12 = 120.
So, the required number is of the form $120k + 2$.
Least value of k for which ($120k + 2$) is divisible by 13 is k = 8.
\ Required number = ($120\times8 + 2$) = 962.

questions.question_text: The traffic lights at three different road crossings change after every 48 sec., 72 sec. and 108 sec. respectively. If they all change simultaneously at 8:20:00 hours, then at what time will they again change simultaneously?
questions.solution:
Interval of change = (L.C.M. of 48, 72, 108) sec. = 432 sec.
So, the lights will again change simultaneously after every 432 seconds, i.e., 7 min. 12 sec.
Hence, next simultaneous change will take place at 8 : 27 : 12 hrs.

questions.question_text: Seema, Meena and Reema begin to jog around a circular stadium and they complete their revolutions in 54 seconds, 42 seconds and 63 seconds respectively. After how much time will they come together at the starting point?
questions.solution:
L.C.M of 54, 42 and 63 = 378.
So, the three girls will come together at the starting point in 378 seconds i.e., 6 min 18 sec.

questions.question_text: Arrange the fractions $\frac{17}{18}, \frac{31}{36}, \frac{43}{45}, \frac{59}{60}$ in the ascending order.
Find the factors of 330.
questions.solution:
2 | 330
3 | 165
5 | 65
  | 11
$\therefore 330 = 2 \times 3 \times 5 \times 11$

questions.question_text: Find the factors of 1122.
252 can be expressed as a product of primes as
questions.solution: Clearly, $252 = 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 3 \times 7$.

questions.question_text: Which of the following has most number of divisors?
A number n is said to be perfect if the sum of all its divisors (excluding n itself) is equal to n. An example of perfect number is
questions.solution:
n | Divisors excluding n | Sum of divisors
6 | 1, 2, 3 | 6
9 | 1, 3 | 4
15 | 1, 3, 5 | 9
21 | 1, 3, 7 | 11
Clearly, 6 is a perfect number.

questions.question_text: $\frac{1095}{1168}$ when expressed in simplest form is
Reduce $\frac{128352}{238368}$ to its lowest terms.
The simplest reduction to the lowest terms of $\frac{116,690,151}{427,863,887}$ is
The highest common factor of 0 and 6 is
questions.solution:
Since division by 0 is undefined, so 0 cannot be a factor of any natural number.
Hence, H.C.F. of 0 and 6 is undefined.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of $2^2 \times 3^3 \times 5^5$, $2^3 \times 3^2 \times 5^2 \times 7$ and $2^4 \times 3^4 \times 5 \times 7^2 \times 11$ is
questions.solution:
H.C.F. = Product of lowest powers of common factors
$= 2^2 \times 3^2 \times 5$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of $2^4 \times 3^2 \times 5^3 \times 7$, $2^3 \times 3^3 \times 5^2 \times 7^2$ and $3 \times 5 \times 7 \times 11$ is
questions.solution:
H.C.F. = Product of lowest powers of common factors
$= 3 \times 5 \times 7 = 105$.

questions.question_text: H.C.F. of $4 \times 27 \times 3125$, $8 \times 9 \times 25 \times 7$ & $16 \times 81 \times 5 \times 11 \times 49$ is
Find the highest common factor of 36 and 84.
questions.solution:
$36 = 2^2 \times 3^2$; $84 = 2^2 \times 3 \times 7$.
$\therefore$ H.C.F. $= 2^2 \times 3 = 12$.

questions.question_text: Even numbers are formed by taking at least two at a time from the numbers 0, 4, 8, 9. Their H.C.F. is
questions.solution:
Since all the numbers formed are even, 2 is a common factor.
Also, H.C.F. of two of the numbers i.e., 48 and 490, is 2.
So, the H.C.F. of all the numbers formed is 2.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of 204, 1190 and 1445 is
Which of the following is a pair of co-primes?
questions.solution: H.C.F. of 18 and 25 is 1. So, they are co-primes.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of 2923 and 3239 is
The H.C.F. of 3556 and 3444 is
The L.C.M. of $2^3 \times 3^2 \times 5 \times 11$, $2^4 \times 3^4 \times 5^2 \times 7$ and $2^5 \times 3^3 \times 5^3 \times 7^2 \times 11$ is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. = Product of highest powers of prime factors =
$2^5 \times 3^4 \times 5^3 \times 7^2 \times 11$.

questions.question_text: Find the lowest common multiple of 24, 36 and 40.
The L.C.M. of 22, 54, 108, 135 and 198 is
The L.C.M. of 148 and 185 is
questions.solution: H.C.F. of 148 and 185 is 37. $\therefore$ L.C.M. = $\left(\frac{148\times185}{37}\right) = 740$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of $\frac{a}{b}, \frac{c}{d}, \frac{e}{f}$ is equal to
questions.option_a: $\frac{\text{L.C.M. of } a, c, e}{\text{H.C.F. of } b, d, f}$
questions.option_b: $\frac{\text{H.C.F. of } a, c, e}{\text{L.C.M. of } b, d, f}$
questions.option_c: $\frac{\text{H.C.F. of } a, c, e}{\text{H.C.F. of } b, d, f}$
questions.solution:
H.C.F. of fractions = $\frac{\text{H.C.F. of Numerators}}{\text{L.C.M. of Denominators}}$.
H.C.F. of $\frac{a}{b}, \frac{c}{d}, \frac{e}{f} = \frac{\text{H.C.F. of } a, c, e}{\text{L.C.M. of } b, d, f}$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of $\frac{2}{3}, \frac{8}{9}, \frac{64}{81}$ and $\frac{10}{27}$ is :
questions.solution: Required H.C.F. = $\frac{\text{H.C.F. of } 2, 8, 64, 10}{\text{L.C.M. of } 3, 9, 81, 27} = \frac{2}{81}$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of $\frac{1}{3}, \frac{5}{6}, \frac{2}{9}, \frac{4}{27}$ is
questions.solution: Required L.C.M. = $\frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 1, 5, 2, 4}{\text{H.C.F. of } 3, 6, 9, 27} = \frac{20}{3}$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of $\frac{2}{3}, \frac{3}{5}, \frac{4}{7}, \frac{9}{13}$ is
questions.solution: Required L.C.M. = $\frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 2, 3, 4, 9}{\text{H.C.F. of } 3, 5, 7, 13} = \frac{36}{1} = 36$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of $\frac{3}{4}, \frac{6}{7}, \frac{9}{8}$ is
questions.solution: Required L.C.M. = $\frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 3, 6, 9}{\text{H.C.F. of } 4, 7, 8} = \frac{18}{1} = 18$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of 1.75, 5.6 and 7 is
questions.solution:
Given numbers with two decimal places are: 1.75, 5.60 and 7.00. Without decimal places, these numbers are: 175, 560 and 700, whose H.C.F. is 35.
$\therefore$ H.C.F. of given numbers = 0.35.

questions.question_text: The G.C.D. of 1.08, 0.36 and 0.9 is
questions.solution:
Given numbers are 1.08, 0.36 and 0.90. H.C.F. of 108, 36 and 90 is 18.
$\therefore$ H.C.F. of given numbers = 0.18.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of 0.54, 1.8 and 7.2 is
questions.solution:
Given numbers are 0.54, 1.80 and 7.20. H.C.F. of 54, 180 and 720 is 18.
$\therefore$ H.C.F. of given numbers = 0.18.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of 3, 2.7 and 0.09 is
questions.solution:
Given numbers are 3.00, 2.70 and 0.09. L.C.M. of 300, 270 and 9 is 2700.
$\therefore$ L.C.M. of given numbers = 27.00 = 27.

questions.question_text: If A, B and C are three numbers, such that the L.C.M. of A and B is B and the L.C.M of B and C is C, then the L.C.M. of A, B and C is
questions.solution: L.C.M. of A and B is B; L.C.M. of B and C is C $\Rightarrow$ L.C.M. of A, B and C is C.

questions.question_text: H.C.F. of 3240, 3600 and a third number is 36 and their L.C.M. is $2^4 \times 3^5 \times 5^2 \times 7^2$. The third number is
questions.solution:
$3240 = 2^3 \times 3^4 \times 5$; $3600 = 2^4 \times 3^2 \times 5^2$;
H.C.F. = $36 = 2^2 \times 3^2$.
Since H.C.F. is the product of lowest powers of common factors, so the third number must have $(2^2 \times 3^2)$ as its factor.
Since L.C.M. is the product of highest powers of common prime factors, so the third number must have $3^5$ and $7^2$ as its factors.
$\therefore$ Third number = $2^2 \times 3^5 \times 7^2$.

questions.question_text: Three numbers are in the ratio 1: 2: 3 and their H.C.F. is 12. The numbers are
questions.solution:
Let the required numbers be $x, 2x$ and $3x$. Then, their H.C.F. = $x$. So, $x = 12$.
$\therefore$ The numbers are 12, 24 and 36.

questions.question_text: The ratio of two numbers is 3: 4 and their H.C.F. is 4. Their L.C.M. is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $3x$ and $4x$. Then, their H.C.F. = $x$. So, $x = 4$.
So, the numbers are 12 and 16.
L.C.M. of 12 and 16 = 48.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 216 and their H.C.F. is 27. The numbers are
questions.solution:
Let the required numbers be $27a$ and $27b$.
Then, $27a + 27b = 216 \Rightarrow a + b = 8$.
Now, co-primes with sum 8 are (1, 7) and (3, 5).
$\therefore$ Required numbers are $(27 \times 1, 27 \times 7)$ and $(27 \times 3, 27 \times 5)$ i.e., (27, 189) and (81, 135).
Out of these, the one available in the given alternatives is the pair (27, 189).

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 528 and their H.C.F. is 33. The number of pairs of numbers satisfying the above conditions is
questions.solution:
Let the required numbers be $33a$ and $33b$.
Then, $33a + 33b = 528 \Rightarrow a + b = 16$.
Now, co-primes with sum 16 are (1, 15), (3, 13), (5, 11) and (7, 9).
$\therefore$ Required numbers are $(33 \times 1, 33 \times 15), (33 \times 3, 33 \times 13), (33 \times 5, 33 \times 11), (33 \times 7, 33 \times 9)$.
The number of such pairs is 4.

questions.question_text: The number of number-pairs lying between 40 and 100 with their H.C.F. as 15 is
questions.solution:
Numbers with H.C.F. 15 must contain 15 as a factor.
Now, multiples of 15 between 40 and 100 are 45, 60, 75 and 90.
$\therefore$ Number-pairs with H.C.F. 15 are (45, 60), (45, 75), (60, 75) and (75, 90).
[Q H.C.F. of (60, 90) is 30 and that of (45, 90) is 45]
Clearly, there are 4 such pairs.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of two numbers is 12 and their difference is 12. The numbers are
questions.solution: Out of the given numbers, the two with H.C.F. 12 and difference 12 are 84 and 96.

questions.question_text: The product of two numbers is 4107. If the H.C.F. of these numbers is 37, then the greater number is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $37a$ and $37b$.
Then, $37a \times 37b = 4107 \Rightarrow ab = 3$.
Now, co-primes with product $3$ are $(1, 3)$.
So, the required numbers are $(37 \times 1, 37 \times 3)$ i.e., $(1, 111)$.
$\therefore$ Greater number $= 111$.

questions.question_text: The product of two numbers is 2028 and their H.C.F. is 13. The number of such pairs is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $13a$ and $13b$.
Then, $13a \times 13b = 2028 \Rightarrow ab = 12$.
Now, co-primes with product $12$ are $(1, 12)$ and $(3, 4)$.
So, the required numbers are $(13 \times 1, 13 \times 12)$ and $(13 \times 3, 13 \times 4)$.
Clearly, there are $2$ such pairs.

questions.question_text: Three numbers which are co-prime to each other are such that the product of the first two is 551 and that of the last two is 1073. The sum of the three numbers is
The ratio of two numbers is 13: 15 and their L.C.M. is 39780. The numbers are
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $13x$ and $15x$.
Then, their L.C.M. $= 195x$.
So, $195x = 39780$ or $x = 204$.
$\therefore$ The numbers are $2652$ and $3060$.

questions.question_text: Three numbers are in the ratio of 3: 4: 5 and their L.C.M. is 2400. Their H.C.F. is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $3x, 4x$ and $5x$.
Then, their L.C.M. $= 60x$.
So, $60x = 2400$ or $x = 40$.
$\therefore$ The numbers are $(3 \times 40), (4 \times 40)$ and $(5 \times 40)$.
Hence, required H.C.F. $= 40$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. and ratio of four numbers are 630 and 2: 3: 5: 7 respectively. The difference between the greatest and least numbers is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $2x, 3x, 5x$ and $7x$ respectively.
Then, their L.C.M. $= (2 \times 3 \times 5 \times 7)x = 210x$.
$[\because 2, 3, 5, 7$ are prime numbers$]$
So, $210x = 630$ or $x = 3$.
$\therefore$ The numbers are $6, 9, 15$ and $21$.
Required difference $= 21 - 6 = 15$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. and L.C.M. of two numbers are 12 and 336 respectively. If one of the numbers is 84, the other is
questions.solution: Other number = $\left(\frac{12 \times 336}{84}\right) = 48$.

questions.question_text: If the product of two numbers is 324 and their H.C.F. is 3, then their L.C.M. will be
questions.solution: L.C.M. $= \left(\frac{324}{3}\right) = 108$.

questions.question_text: If H.C.F. of p and q is x and q = xy, then the L.C.M. of p and q is
questions.solution:
Product of numbers = H.C.F. $\times$ L.C.M.
$\Rightarrow pq = x \times \text{L.C.M.}$
$\Rightarrow \text{L.C.M.} = \left(\frac{pq}{x}\right) = \left(\frac{p \times xy}{x}\right) = py$.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 2000 and their L.C.M. is 21879. The two numbers are
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $(2000 - x)$.
Then, their L.C.M. $= x(2000 - x)$.
So, $x(2000 - x) = 21879$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 - 2000x + 21879 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow (x - 1989)(x - 11) = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow x = 1989$ or $x = 11$.
Hence, the numbers are $1989$ and $11$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. and L.C.M. of two numbers are 84 and 21 respectively. If the ratio of the two numbers is 1: 4, then the larger of the two numbers is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $4x$.
Then, $x \times 4x = 84 \times 21 \Leftrightarrow x^2 = \left(\frac{84 \times 21}{4}\right) \Leftrightarrow x = 21$.
Hence, larger number $= 4x = 84$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of two numbers is $495$ and their H.C.F. is $5$. If the sum of the numbers is $10$, then their difference is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $(100 - x)$.
Then, $x(100 - x) = 5 \times 495$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 - 100x + 2475 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow (x - 55)(x - 45) = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow x = 55$ or $x = 45$.
$\therefore$ The numbers are $45$ and $55$.
Required difference $= (55 - 45) = 10$.

questions.question_text: The product of the L.C.M. and H.C.F. of two numbers is $24$. The difference of two numbers is $2$. Find the numbers.
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $(x + 2)$.
Then, $x(x + 2) = 24$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 + 2x - 24 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow (x - 4)(x + 6) = 0 \Leftrightarrow x = 4$.
So, the numbers are $4$ and $6$.

questions.question_text: If the sum of two numbers is $36$ and their H.C.F. and L.C.M. are $3$ and $105$ respectively, the sum of the reciprocals of the two numbers is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $a$ and $b$.
Then, $a + b = 36$ and $ab = 3 \times 105 = 315$.
$\therefore$ Required sum $= \left(\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}\right) = \left(\frac{a + b}{ab}\right) = \left(\frac{36}{315}\right) = \frac{4}{35}$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of two numbers is $12$ times their H.C.F. The sum of H.C.F. and L.C.M. is $403$. If one number is $93$, find the other.
questions.solution:
Let H.C.F be $h$ and L.C.M. be $l$.
Then, $l = 12h$ and $l + h = 403$.
$\therefore 12h + h = 403$ or $h = 31$.
So $l = (403 - 31) = 372$.
Hence, other number $= \left(\frac{31 \times 372}{93}\right) = 124$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. and L.C.M. of two numbers are $50$ and $250$ respectively. If the first number is divided by $2$, the quotient is $50$. The second number is
questions.solution:
First number $= (50 \times 2) = 100$.
Second number $= \left(\frac{50 \times 250}{100}\right) = 125$.

questions.question_text: The product of two numbers is $1320$ and their H.C.F. is $6$. The L.C.M. of the numbers is
questions.solution: L.C.M. $= \left(\frac{\text{Product of numbers}}{\text{H.C.F.}}\right) = \left(\frac{1320}{6}\right) = 220$.

questions.question_text: Product of two co-prime numbers is $117$. Their L.C.M. should be
questions.solution: H.C.F. of co-prime numbers is $1$. So, L.C.M. $= \left(\frac{117}{1}\right) = 117$.

questions.question_text: The L.C.M. of three different numbers is $120$. Which of the following cannot be their H.C.F.?
questions.solution: Since H.C.F. is always a factor of L.C.M., we cannot have three numbers with H.C.F. $35$ and L.C.M. $120$.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of two numbers is $8$. Which one of the following can never be their L.C.M.?
questions.solution: H.C.F. of two numbers divides their L.C.M. exactly. Clearly, $8$ is not a factor of $60$.

questions.question_text: If the L.C.M. of three numbers is $9570$, then their H.C.F. can be
questions.solution:
The factors of 9570 are 2, 3, 5, 11 and 29.
Clearly, H.C.F. can be any of these factors occurring not more than once and no number other than 2, 3, 5, 11 or 29 or having any factor other than these, can be the H.C.F.
So, the only possibility is 11.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. of two numbers is $23$ and the other two factors of their L.C.M. are $13$ and $14$. The larger of the two numbers is
questions.solution:
Clearly, the numbers are $(23	imes13)$ and $(23	imes14)$.
$\therefore$ Larger number = $(23	imes14) = 322$.

questions.question_text: About the number of pairs which have $16$ as their H.C.F. and $136$ as their L.C.M., we can definitely say that
questions.solution: Since 16 is not a factor of 136, it follows that there does not exist any pair of numbers with H.C.F. 16 and L.C.M. 136.

questions.question_text: The H.C.F. and L.C.M. of two numbers are $21$ and $4641$ respectively. If one of the numbers lies between $200$ and $300$, the two numbers are
questions.solution:
Product of numbers = $21	imes4641 = 97461$.
Let the numbers be $21a$ and $21b$.
Then, $21a	imes21b = 97461 \Rightarrow ab = 221$.
Now, co-primes with product 221 are (1, 221) and (13, 17).
So, the numbers are $(21	imes1, 21	imes221)$ and $(21	imes13, 21	imes17)$.
Since one number lies between 200 and 300, the suitable pair is (273, 357).

questions.question_text: Two numbers, both greater than $29$, have H.C.F. $29$ and L.C.M. $4147$. The sum of the numbers is
questions.solution:
Product of numbers = $29	imes4147$.
Let the numbers be $29a$ and $29b$.
Then, $29a	imes29b = (29	imes4147) \Rightarrow ab = 143$.
Now, co-primes with product 143 are (1, 143) and (11, 13).
So, the numbers are $(29	imes1, 29	imes143)$ and $(29	imes11, 29	imes13)$.
Since both numbers are greater than 29, the suitable pair is $(29	imes11, 29	imes13)$, i.e., (319, 377).
$\therefore$ Required sum = $(319+377) = 696$.

questions.question_text: L.C.M. of two prime numbers $x$ and $y$ ($x > y$) is $161$. The value of $3y - x$ is
questions.solution:
H.C.F. of two prime numbers is 1.
Product of numbers = $(1	imes161) = 161$.
Let the numbers be $a$ and $b$. Then, $ab = 161$.
Now, co-primes with product 161 are (1, 161) and (7, 23).
Since $x$ and $y$ are prime numbers and $x > y$, we have $x = 23$ and $y = 7$.
$\therefore 3y - x = (3	imes7) - 23 = -2$.

questions.question_text: The greatest number that exactly divides $105, 1001$ and $2436$ is
questions.solution:
H.C.F. of 2436 and 1001 is 7. Also, H.C.F. of 105 and 7 is 7.
$\therefore$ H.C.F. of 105, 1001 and 2436 is 7.

questions.question_text: $21$ mango trees, $42$ apple trees and $56$ orange trees have to be planted in rows such that each row contains the same number of trees of one variety only. Minimum number of rows in which the trees may be planted is
questions.solution:
For the minimum number of rows, the number of trees in each row must be the maximum.
$\therefore$ Number of trees in each row = H.C.F. of 21, 42, 56 = 7.
Hence, number of rows
$=  rac{21+42+56}{7} =  rac{119}{7} = 17$.

questions.question_text: The greatest possible length which can be used to measure exactly the lengths $7$ m, $3$ m $85$ cm, $12$ m $95$ cm is
questions.solution: Required length = H.C.F. of 700 cm, 385 cm and 1295 cm = 35 cm.

questions.question_text: The capacity of two pots is $120$ litres and $56$ litres respectively. Find the capacity of a container which can exactly measure the contents of the two pots.
questions.solution:
Required capacity
= H.C.F. of 120 litres and 56 litres
= 8 litres = 8000 cc. [$\because$ 1 litre = 1000 cc]

questions.question_text: A daily wage labourer was engaged for a certain number of days for ₹ $5750$, but being absent on some of those days he was paid only ₹ $5000$. What was his maximum possible daily wage?
questions.solution: Maximum possible daily wage = H.C.F. of ₹ 5750 and ₹ 5000 = ₹ 250.

questions.passage_text: A person has to completely put each of three liquids: $403$ litres of petrol, $465$ litres of diesel and $496$ litres of Mobil Oil in bottles of equal size without mixing any of the above three types of liquids such that each bottle is completely filled. What is the least possible number of bottles required?
questions.question_text: What is the least possible number of bottles required?
questions.solution:
For the least number of bottles, the capacity of each bottle must be maximum.
$\therefore$ Capacity of each bottle = H.C.F. of 403 litres, 465 litres and 496 litres = 31 litres.
Hence, required number of bottles
$=  rac{403+465+496}{31} =  rac{1364}{31} = 44$.

questions.question_text: The maximum number of students among whom $1001$ pens and $910$ pencils can be distributed in such a way that each student gets the same number of pens and same number of pencils is
questions.solution: Required number of students = H.C.F. of 1001 and 910 = 91.

questions.question_text: A rectangular courtyard $3.78$ metres long and $5.25$ metres wide is to be paved exactly with square tiles, all of the same size. What is the largest size of the tile which could be used for the purpose?
questions.solution: Largest size of the tile = H.C.F. of 378 cm and 525 cm = 21 cm.

questions.question_text: The least number of square tiles required to pave the ceiling of a room $15$ m $17$ cm long and $9$ m $2$ cm broad is
questions.solution:
For the least number of tiles, the size of the tile must be the maximum.
Maximum size of the tile = H.C.F. of 1517 cm and 902 cm = 41 cm.
Hence, required number of tiles
$=  rac{1517	imes902}{41	imes41} = 814$.

questions.question_text: Three sets of English, Mathematics and Science books containing $336, 240$ and $96$ books respectively have to be stacked in such a way that all the books are stored subjectwise and the height of each stack is the same. Total number of stacks will be
questions.solution:
Number of books in each stack = H.C.F. of 336, 240 and 96 = 48.
Hence, total number of stacks = $ rac{336+240+96}{48} =  rac{672}{48} = 14$.

questions.question_text: Four metal rods of lengths $78$ cm, $104$ cm, $117$ cm and $169$ cm are to be cut into parts of equal length. Each part must be as long as possible. What is the maximum number of pieces that can be cut?
questions.solution:
Maximum length of each part = H.C.F. of 78 cm, 104 cm, 117 cm, 169 cm = 13 cm.
$\therefore$ Number of pieces =
$ rac{78+104+117+169}{13} =  rac{468}{13} = 36$.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest number that will divide $43, 91$ and $183$ so as to leave the same remainder in each case.
questions.solution:
Required number
= H.C.F. of (91 - 43), (183 - 91) and (183 - 43)
= H.C.F. of 48, 92 and 140 = 4.

questions.question_text: If $r$ is the remainder when each of $7654, 8506$ and $9997$ is divided by the greatest number $d$ ($d > 1$), then $d - r$ is equal to
Let N be the greatest number that will divide $1305, 4665$ and $6905$, leaving the same remainder in each case. Then sum of the digits in N is

A milkman has $3$ jars containing $57$ litres, $129$ litres and $177$ litres of pure milk respectively. A measuring can, after a different number of exact measurements of milk in each jar, leaves the same amount of milk unmeasured in each jar. What is the volume of the largest such can?
questions.solution:
Required volume
$= [H.C.F. of (129 - 57), (177 - 129)
and (177 - 57)]$ litres
$= (H.C.F. of 72, 48 and 120)$ litres $= 24$ litres.

questions.question_text: The greatest number which can divide $1356, 1868$ and $2764$ leaving the same remainder $12$ in each case is
Which greatest number will divide $3026$ and $5053$ leaving remainders $11$ and $13$ respectively?
questions.solution:
Required number
$= H.C.F. of (3026 - 11) and (5053 - 13)$
$= H.C.F. of 3015 and 5040 = 45.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest number that will divide $964, 1238$ and $1400$ leaving remainders $41, 31$ and $51$ respectively.
Which of the following fractions is the largest?
What is the least natural number which leaves no remainder when divided by all the digits from $1$ to $9$?
questions.solution:
Required number
$= L.C.M. of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9$
$= 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 5 \times 7 \times 2 \times 3 = 2520$.

questions.question_text: What will be the least number which when doubled will be exactly divisible by $12, 18, 21$ and $30$?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 12, 18, 21, 30
$= 2 \times 3 \times 2 \times 3 \times 7 \times 5$
$= 1260$.
$\therefore$ Required number $= (1260 \div 2) = 630$.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is $45$. Their difference is $\frac{1}{9}$ of their sum. Their L.C.M. is
questions.solution:
Let the two numbers be $a$ and $b$.
Then, $a + b = 45$ ...(i)
And, $a - b = 5$ ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get: $2a = 50$ or $a = 25$.
Putting $a = 25$ in (i), we get: $b = 20$.
$\therefore$ L.C.M. $= 5 \times 4 \times 5 = 100$.

questions.question_text: The smallest fraction, which each of $\frac{6}{7}, \frac{5}{14}, \frac{10}{21}$ will divide exactly is
questions.solution:
Required fraction
$= \frac{L.C.M. of 6, 5, 10}{H.C.F. of 7, 14, 21} = \frac{30}{7}$.

questions.question_text: The least number of five digits which is exactly divisible by $12, 15$ and $18$ is
questions.solution:
Least number of 5 digits is 10000. L.C.M. of 12, 15 and 18 is 180.
On dividing 10000 by 180, the remainder is 100.
$\therefore$ Required number $= 10000 + (180 - 100) = 10080$.

questions.question_text: The greatest number of four digits which is divisible by $15, 25, 40$ and $75$ is
questions.solution:
Greatest number of 4 digits is 9999.
L.C.M. of 15, 25, 40 and 75 $= 5 \times 5 \times 3 \times 8 = 600$.
On dividing 9999 by 600, the remainder is 399.
$\therefore$ Required number $= (9999 - 399) = 9600$.

questions.question_text: The number between $4000$ and $5000$ which is divisible by $12, 18, 21$ and $32$ is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 12, 18, 21 and 32 $= 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 3 \times 7 \times 8 = 2016$.
So, the required number is a multiple of 2016 and lies between 4000 and 5000.
Hence, required number $= 4032$.

questions.question_text: The number nearest to $43582$ divisible by each of $25, 50$ and $75$ is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 25, 50 and 75 $= 5 \times 5 \times 2 \times 3 = 150$.
On dividing 43582 by 150, the remainder is 82 and quotient is 290.
So, required number $= 150 \times 291 = 43650$.

questions.question_text: The least number which should be added to $2497$ so that the sum is exactly divisible by $5, 6, 4$ and $3$ is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 5, 6, 4 and 3 = 60. On dividing 2497 by 60, the remainder is 37.
$\therefore$ Number to be added $= (60 - 37) = 23$.

questions.question_text: The greatest number which when subtracted from $5834$, gives a number exactly divisible by each of $20, 28, 32$ and $35$ is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 20, 28, 32, 35 $= 2 \times 2 \times 5 \times 7 \times 8 = 1120$.
Required number $= (5834 - 1120) = 4714$.

questions.question_text: The least number which is a perfect square and is divisible by each of the numbers $16, 20$ and $24$, is
questions.solution:
The least number divisible by 16, 20, 24
$= L.C.M. of 16, 20, 24 = 240 = 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 5$.
To make it a perfect square, it must be multiplied by $3 \times 5$.
$\therefore$ Required number $= 240 \times 3 \times 5 = 3600$.

questions.question_text: The smallest number which when diminished by $7$, is divisible by $12, 16, 18, 21$ and $28$ is
The least number which when increased by $5$ is divisible by each one of $24, 32, 36$ and $54$ is
The least number, which when divided by $12, 15, 20$ and $54$ leaves in each case a remainder of $8$ is
A number less than $500$, when divided by $4, 5, 6, 7$ leaves remainder $1$ in each case. The number is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 4, 5, 6, 7 = 420.
$\therefore$ Required number $= 420 + 1 = 421$.

questions.question_text: What is the greatest number of $3$ digits which when divided by $6, 9$ and $12$ leaves a remainder of $3$ in each case?
questions.solution:
Greatest number of 3 digits is 999. L.C.M. of 6, 9 and 12 is 36.
On dividing 999 by 36, the remainder obtained is 27.
So, required number = $(999 - 27) + 3 = 975$.

questions.question_text: The largest four-digit number which when divided by $4, 7$ or $13$ leaves a remainder of $3$ in each case, is
questions.solution:
Greatest number of 4 digits is 9999. L.C.M. of 4, 7 and 13 = 364.
On dividing 9999 by 364, the remainder obtained is 171.
$\therefore$ Greatest number of 4 digits divisible by 4, 7 and 13
$= (9999 - 171) = 9828$.
Hence, required number = $(9828 + 3) = 9831$.

questions.question_text: Let the least number of six digits, which when divided by $4, 6, 10$ and $15$, leaves in each case the same remainder of $2$, be N. The sum of the digits in N is
questions.solution:
Least number of 6 digits is 100000. L.C.M. of 4, 6, 10 and 15 = 60.
On dividing 100000 by 60, the remainder obtained is 40.
$\therefore$ Least number of 6 digits divisible by 4, 6, 10 and 15
$= 100000 + (60 - 40) = 100020$.
$\therefore N = (100020 + 2) = 100022$.
Sum of digits in N = $(1 + 2 + 2) = 5$.

questions.question_text: The least multiple of $13$, which on dividing by $4, 5, 6, 7$ and $8$ leaves remainder $2$ in each case is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 is 840.
Let the required number be $840k + 2$, which is a multiple of 13.
Least value of $k$ for which $(840k + 2)$ is divisible by 13 is $k = 3$.
$\therefore$ Required number = $840 \times 3 + 2 = 2522$.

questions.question_text: Find the least number which when divided by $12$, leaves a remainder of $7$; when divided by $15$, leaves a remainder of $10$ and when divided by $16$, leaves a remainder of $11$.
The least number, which when divided by $48, 60, 72, 108$ and $140$ leaves $38, 50, 62, 98$ and $130$ as remainders respectively is
Find the least multiple of $23$, which when divided by $18, 21$ and $24$ leaves remainders $7, 10$ and $13$ respectively.
questions.solution:
Here $(18 - 7) = 11, (21 - 10) = 11$ and $(24 - 13) = 11$. L.C.M. of 18, 21 and 24 is 504.
Let the required number be $504k - 11$.
Least value of $k$ for which $(504k - 11)$ is divisible by 23 is $k = 6$.
$\therefore$ Required number = $504 \times 6 - 11 = 3024 - 11 = 3013$.

questions.question_text: What is the third term in a sequence of numbers that leave remainders of $1, 2$ and $3$ when divided by $2, 3$ and $4$ respectively?
questions.solution:
Clearly, $(2 - 1) = 1, (3 - 2) = 1$ and $(4 - 3) = 1$.
L.C.M. of 2, 3, 4 = 12.
So, the sequence shall have numbers of the form $12k - 1$, where $k = 1, 2, 3, \dots$
$\therefore$ Third term of the sequence = $12 \times 3 - 1 = 36 - 1 = 35$.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest number of $4$ digits which when divided by $4, 5, 6, 7$ and $8$ leaves $1, 2, 3, 4$ and $5$ as remainders.
questions.solution:
Clearly, $(4 - 1) = 3, (5 - 2) = 3, (6 - 3) = 3, (7 - 4) = 3$ and $(8 - 5) = 3$.
L.C.M. of 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 = 840.
Greatest number of 4 digits = 9999.
On dividing 9999 by 840, the remainder is 759.
So, required number = $(9999 - 759) - 3 = 9237$.

questions.question_text: The least number which when divided by $5, 6, 7$ and $8$ leaves a remainder $3$, but when divided by $9$ leaves no remainder, is
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 5, 6, 7, 8 = 840.
$\therefore$ Required number is of the form $840k + 3$.
Least value of $k$ for which $(840k + 3)$ is divisible by 9 is $k = 2$.
$\therefore$ Required number = $(840 \times 2 + 3) = 1683$.

questions.question_text: Find the least number which when divided by $16, 18, 20$ and $25$ leaves $4$ as remainder in each case, but when divided by $7$ leaves no remainder.
questions.solution:
L.C.M of 16, 18, 20, 25 = 3600.
Required number is of the form $3600k + 4$.
Least value of $k$ for which $(3600k + 4)$ is divisible by 7 is $k = 5$.
$\therefore$ Required number = $(3600 \times 5 + 4) = 18004$.

questions.question_text: A gardener has to plant trees in rows containing equal number of trees. If he plants in rows of $6, 8, 10$ or $12$, then five trees are left unplanted. But if he plants in rows of $13$ trees each, then no tree is left. What is the number of trees that the gardener plants?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 6, 8, 10, 12 = 120.
$\therefore$ Required number is of the form $120k + 5$.
Least value of $k$ for which $(120k + 5)$ is divisible by 13 is $k = 7$.
$\therefore$ Required number = $(120 \times 7 + 5) = 845$.

questions.question_text: When Seeta made necklaces of either $16$ beads, $20$ beads or $36$ beads, not a single bead was left over. What could be the least number of beads Seeta had?
questions.solution: Required number of beads = L.C.M. of 16, 20, 36 = 720.

questions.question_text: An electronic device makes a beep after every 60 sec. Another device makes a beep after every 62 sec. They beeped together at 10 a.m. The next time, when they would beep together at the earliest is
questions.solution:
Interval of change = (L.C.M. of 60 and 62) sec = 1860 sec = 31 min.
So, the devices would beep together 31 min after 10 a.m., i.e., at 10.31 a.m.

questions.question_text: Six bells commence tolling together and toll at intervals of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 and 12 seconds respectively. In 30 minutes, how many times do they toll together?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 is 120.
So, the bells will toll together after every 120 seconds, i.e., 2 minutes.
In 30 minutes, they will toll together $\left[\frac{30}{2}\right] + 1 = 16$ times.

questions.question_text: Four bells begin to toll together and toll respectively at intervals of 6, 7, 8 and 9 seconds. In 1.54 hours, how many times do they toll together and in what interval (seconds)?
questions.solution:
Interval after which the bells will toll together
= (L.C.M. of 6, 7, 8, 9) sec = 504 sec.
In 1.54 hours, they will toll together $\left[\frac{1.54 \times 60 \times 60}{504}\right] + 1$ times = 12 times.

questions.question_text: Four different electronic devices make a beep after every 30 minutes, 1 hour, $1\frac{1}{2}$ hour and 1 hour 45 minutes respectively. All the devices beeped together at 12 noon. They will again beep together at
questions.solution:
Interval after which the devices will beep together
= (L.C.M. of 30, 60, 90, 105) min.
= 1260 min = 21 hrs.
So, the devices will again beep together 21 hrs. after 12 noon i.e., at 9 a.m.

questions.question_text: Three girls start jogging from the same point around a circular track and each one completes one round in 24 seconds, 36 seconds and 48 seconds respectively. After how much time will they meet at one point?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 24, 36, 48 = 144.
So, the three girls will meet at one point in 144 seconds i.e., 2 min 24 sec.

questions.question_text: Three persons walking around a circular track complete their respective single revolutions in $1\frac{15}{6}$ seconds, $1\frac{16}{4}$ seconds and $2\frac{18}{3}$ seconds respectively. They will be again together at the common starting point after an hour and
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of $\frac{91}{6}, \frac{65}{4}$ and $\frac{56}{3} = \frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 91, 65, 56}{\text{H.C.F. of } 6, 4, 3} = 3640$.
So, the three persons will be together at the starting point in 3640 sec
i.e., 1 hour 40 seconds.

questions.question_text: A, B and C start at the same time in the same direction to run around a circular stadium. A completes a round in 252 seconds, B in 308 seconds and C in 198 seconds, all starting at the same point. After what time will they meet again at the starting point?
questions.solution:
L.C.M. of 252, 308 and 198 = 2772.
So, A, B and C will again meet at the starting point in 2772 sec. i.e., 46 min. 12 sec.

questions.question_text: Three wheels can complete 40, 24 and 16 revolutions per minute respectively. There is a red spot on each wheel that touches the ground at time zero. After how much time, all these spots will simultaneously touch the ground again?
questions.solution:
For one complete revolution, the first, second and third wheels take $\frac{60}{40}, \frac{60}{24}, \frac{60}{16}$ seconds i.e., $\frac{3}{2}, \frac{5}{2}, \frac{15}{4}$ seconds respectively.
Time taken for all red spots to touch the ground again simultaneously.
$= \left(\text{L.C.M. of } \frac{3}{2}, \frac{5}{2}, \frac{15}{4}\right) \text{sec} = \left(\frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 3, 5, 15}{\text{H.C.F. of } 2, 2, 4}\right) \text{sec}$
$= \frac{15}{2} \text{sec} = 7\frac{1}{2} \text{sec}$.

questions.question_text: A pendulum strikes 5 times in 3 seconds and another pendulum strikes 7 times in 4 seconds. If both pendulums start striking at the same time, how many clear strikes can be listened in 1 minute?
questions.solution:
First pendulum strikes once in $3$ seconds. Second pendulum strikes once in $\frac{4}{7}$ seconds.
L.C.M. of $\frac{3}{5}$ and $\frac{4}{7} = \frac{\text{L.C.M. of } 3 \text{ and } 4}{\text{H.C.F. of } 5 \text{ and } 7} = 12$.
So, they strike together after every $12$ seconds.
Thus, they strike together $\left(\frac{60}{12} + 1\right) = 6$ times in $1$ minute.
Total number of clear strikes heard
$= \left[\left(\frac{60}{3} + \frac{60}{4/7}\right) - 6\right] = (100 + 105) - 6 = 199$.

questions.question_text: Find the HCF of 132, 204 and 228.
If three numbers are $2a$, $5a$ and $7a$, what will be their LCM?
questions.solution: The given three numbers are $2a, 5a$ and $7a$. LCM of $2a, 5a$ and $7a = 2 \times 5 \times 7 \times a = 70a$

questions.question_text: The product of two whole numbers is 1500 and their HCF is 10. Find the LCM.
questions.solution:
Product of two numbers $= 1500$, HCF $= 10$
$\text{LCM} = \frac{\text{Product of two numbers}}{\text{Their HCF}}$
$= \frac{1500}{10} = 150$
Required LCM is $150$.

questions.question_text: A number $x$ is divided by 7. When this number is divided by 8, 12 and 16. It leaves a remainder 3 in each case. The least value of $x$ is:
questions.solution:
LCM of $8, 12$ and $16 = 48$
$2 \mid 8 - 12 - 16$
$2 \mid 4 - 6 - 8$
$2 \mid 2 - 3 - 4$
$1 - 3 - 2$
$2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 = 48$
$\therefore$ Required number $= 48a + 3$
Which is divisible by $7$.
$\therefore x = 48a + 3 = 7 \times 6a + 6a + 3$
$= (7 \times 6a) + (6a + 3)$ which is divisible by $7$.
i.e. $6a + 3$ is divisible by $7$.
When $a = 3, 6a + 3 = 18 + 3 = 21$ which is divisible by $7$.
$\therefore x = 48 \times 3 + 3 = 144 + 3 = 147$

questions.question_text: The number of pair of positive integers whose sum is 99 and HCF is 9 is
questions.solution: Number of pair of positive integers whose sum is $99$ and HCF is $9$ is $(9, 90); (18, 81); (27, 72); (36, 63); (45, 54)$.

questions.question_text: The ratio of two numbers is 3 : 4 and their LCM is 120. The sum of numbers is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers are $3x$ and $4x$
So, HCF $= x$
$\text{HCF} \times \text{LCM} = \text{Product of numbers}$
$\Rightarrow x \times 120 = 3x \times 4x$
$\Rightarrow x \times 120 = 12x^2$
$\Rightarrow 120 = 12x$
$\Rightarrow x = 10$
$\therefore$ Numbers are $30$ and $40$.
$\therefore$ Sum of two numbers $= 30 + 40 = 70$

questions.question_text: The greatest four digit number which is exactly divisible by each one of the numbers 12, 18, 21 and 28
questions.solution:
The greatest $4$ digit number is $9999$
The LCM of $12, 18, 21, 28$ is $252$
On dividing $9999$ by $252$ the remainder comes out to be $171$
$\therefore$ Required number $= 9999 - 171 = 9828$

questions.question_text: The traffic lights at three different signal points change after every 45 seconds, 75 seconds and 90 seconds respectively. If all change simultaneously at 7 : 20 : 15 hours, then they will change again simultaneously at
questions.solution:
$5 \mid 45 - 75 - 90$
$3 \mid 9 - 15 - 18$
$3 \mid 3 - 5 - 6$
$1 - 5 - 2$
$\Rightarrow 5 \times 3 \times 3 \times 5 \times 2 = 450$
LCM of $45, 75, 90$ is $450$
$\therefore$ Traffic lights will change simultaneously after $7$ minutes $30$ seconds i.e. at $7 : 27 : 45$ hours.
